channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Landeskog 'excited about what the future looks like' after playing in a game for 1st time in 3 years
item.description: LOVELAND, Colo. (AP) — For the first time in 1,020 or so days, Gabriel Landeskog has some fresh game tape to examine. Game tape of him hitting players, taking hits, being called for a penalty and even putting someone in a headlock.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jokic wraps up triple-double average for season, 3rd NBA player to achieve that feat
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Nikola Jokic will average a triple-double for the season, making the Denver star the third player in NBA history to pull off such a feat. It became statistically certain Friday night when Jokic got his fourth assist of Denver’s game against the Memphis Grizzlies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Edwards' 18th technical foul means another suspension, this time for Timberwolves' meaningful finale
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Much of this season for the Minnesota Timberwolves has been harder than it needed to be, so it's fitting they might have to play their final game — which they need to win to improve their seed for the playoffs — without star Anthony Edwards.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cardinals reserve C Yohel Pozo making the most of his first trip back to the majors since 2021
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Reserve catcher Yohel Pozo will remember his first time being in St. Louis. Pozo came off the bench in the second inning against the Philadelphia Phillies on Friday night after teammate Masyn Winn left after one inning with lower back spasms, sliding into the shortstop's No.
item.dc:creator: WARREN MAYES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Knicks shrug off 0-8 record vs Cavaliers and Celtics, but know they don't appear playoff-ready
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The New York Knicks aren't worried about what their 0-8 record against Cleveland and Boston might mean in the playoffs. After all, they know the way they are playing now might not even be good enough to beat Detroit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taylor gets 3 hits as the White Sox stop 8-game slide by routing the Red Sox 11-1
item.description: Michael A. Taylor had three hits and scored three times, and the Chicago White Sox stopped an eight-game slide by routing the sloppy Boston Red Sox 11-1 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rory McIlroy catches fire, shoots 66 to get back into contention at the Masters
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Rory McIlroy’s fragile mental state got a big boost when he left Augusta National on Thursday night, shortly after a pair of late double bogeys had cast a pall on an otherwise brilliant round, in time to put his 4-year-old daughter, Poppy, to bed.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Detroit Pistons fall to Bucks in home finale, will face Knicks in first round of NBA playoffs
item.description: Despite the defeat, the Detroit Pistons will face the New York Knicks in the first round of the playoffs inside Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Warriors embracing ‘pretty crazy’ end to NBA regular season
item.description: Golden State Warriors still are in contention for No. 4 seed, but could also fall to No. 8 with two games to play.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: NFL great Terry Bradshaw clarifies role in opening jammed plane door after claims of heroism
item.description: A passenger traveling on a flight from Dallas-Forth Worth to Pittsburgh Thursday provided a firsthand account of how a malfunctioning plane door was eventually opened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rory McIlroy, Bryson DeChambeau surge; Justin Rose holds slim Masters lead at midway point
item.description: We are in for an amazing finish at Augusta National this weekend after a second round that saw Rory McIlroy and Bryson Dechambeau climb the leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Masters: When it starts, how to watch, betting odds for golf's first major of 2025
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Justin Rose remained atop a star-studded leaderboard after the second round of the Masters on Friday, with defending champion Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau all within three shots of the lead.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Freddie Freeman returns to Dodgers' lineup after missing 9 games with ankle injury from shower slip
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers activated Freddie Freeman off the injured list after he missed nine games with a right ankle injury that resulted from slipping in the shower at home.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blackhawks host the Jets following Foligno's 2-goal game
item.description: Winnipeg Jets (54-21-4, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (23-46-10, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 7 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks host the Winnipeg Jets after Nick Foligno's two-goal game against the Boston Bruins in the Blackhawks' 5-2 win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The NBA's playoff chase has hit the final weekend. Here's a look at what's happening
item.description: There's 15 games on Friday. There's 15 more on Sunday. And there's a whole lot of things left for those last 30 games of the NBA regular season to decide.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salah stays: Egypt star signs 2-year deal at Liverpool and sets sights on more trophies
item.description: Mohamed Salah’s trophy-laden spell with Liverpool is set to extend to a decade after the prolific Egypt forward signed a new contract with the Premier League leader, ending months of uncertainty over his future.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah falls in shootout in home finale, 4-3
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In the final game at the Delta Center in their inaugural season, the Utah Hockey Club lost a thriller in a shootout to the Nashville Predators Thursday night, 4-3.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Michaels lifts Western Michigan to 3-2 2OT Frozen Four semifinal win over defending champion Denver
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Owen Michaels s cored his second goal 26 seconds into the second overtime and Western Michigan beat defending champion Denver 3-2 on Thursday night to advance to its first Frozen Four title game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: BYU lands point guard Robert Wright III from Baylor
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It didn't take long for Kevin Young to find his new point guard. According to multiple reports, Baylor's Robert Wright III is transferring to BYU for his sophomore season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Like father, like son: Shoei Darvish steps on the hill for Dons
item.description: With a flurry of pitches, Shoei Darvish showed the skills that we've already seen in Petco Park from his father. But make no mistake, this junior righty is doing it on his terms - but with some talented influence.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz end 9-game skid with overtime win over Blazers
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In what has been an insufferable season for Utah Jazz fans, the team gave them a treat Wednesday night -- a thrilling overtime victory.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bulls favored to land Nikola Jokic if traded, odds maker says
item.description: If the Denver Nuggets were to trade Nikola Jokic, the Chicago Bulls are the top landing spot according to Bookies.com's Adam Thompson, who gave the Bulls a +500 odds to acquire the 3-time NBA MVP.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Babe Ruth's former Florida home goes up for sale for nearly $2M
item.description: A Florida home with historical ties, including to baseball legend Babe Ruth, hit the market for just under $2 million.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: Utah scores season-high 7 goals in win over Seattle
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Whether or not they make the NHL playoffs, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season with a bang.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jaylon Johnson wants to see real accountability before he says the Bears have changed for the better
item.description: Jaylon Johnson is heading into his sixth NFL season and third go-around riding Chicago's head coaching carousel, and is excited to see the positive change that has eluded them since he joined the franchise, and is looking forward to the Bears' fresh start with new head coach Ben Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Loyola University Chicago sent preservation notice tied to federally indicted ex-Michigan coach
item.description: Loyola University Chicago has received a preservation notice from attorneys representing a former student-athlete, compelling the school to preserve evidence related to the indictment of former Michigan football assistant coach Matt Weiss on unauthorized computer access and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Egor Demin leaves BYU for NBA Draft
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU standout point guard Egor Demin has decided to leave BYU after one season and declare for the NBA Draft. Demin made the announcement at a press conference held at the school Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Northwestern University to settle lawsuit over football program hazing allegations
item.description: Northwestern University has agreed to settle a lawsuit filed by students against the school and former head coach Pat Fitzgerald, who alleged hazing incidents took place in the football program, but the settlement does not resolve Fitzgerald's lawsuit against the university.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Steele tosses 7 innings as the Cubs pound the Rangers 7-0 on frigid night
item.description: Justin Steele tossed three-hit ball over seven innings, and the Chicago Cubs beat the Texas Rangers 7-0 on a frigid Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Johnny Gaudreau's wife Meredith announces birth of couple's third child
item.description: Johnny and his brother were killed in New Jersey when they were riding bicycles and struck from behind by a suspected drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Torkelson's 2-run double in the ninth inning gives Tigers 4-3 win over White Sox
item.description: Spencer Torkelson smacked a two-run double to cap Detroit's three-run rally in the ninth inning and give the Tigers a 4-3 win over the Chicago White Sox on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin fired just about the perfect version of his signature one-timer that has defined his remarkable career. When the puck hit the net, it made him the top goal scorer in NHL history.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, NewsNation
